libertadEmocional.title: Emotional Freedom
libertadEmocional.description: A 4-week study to experience healing and spiritual growth through biblical truth, especially if you have experienced abuse, deep wounds, or confusion about your identity.

s1.title: Week 1: Understanding Negative Emotions and Their Origin

d2.title: Day 2: The origin of negative emotions

s2.title: Week 2: Healing the Wounds of the Past

d6.title: Day 6: Identify the wounds behind your emotions

d7.title: Day 7: Revisit with purpose, not pain

d10.title: Day 10: Say goodbye to the past

d11.title: Day 11: Identify the lies you have believed

d15.title: Day 15: Recover what the enemy stole

s4.title: Week 4: Living in Freedom and Truth

d16.title: Day 16: Break free from your story's control

d17.title: Day 17: Accept the consequences with hope

d18.title: Day 18: Live as a trophy of God's healing

d19.title: Day 19: Help others in their freedom journey

d20.title: Day 20: Proclaim your freedom every day

consejeria_matrimonial.title: Christian Marriage Counseling
consejeria_matrimonial.desc: A 6-lesson biblical study on the foundations of marriage: communication, forgiveness, roles, intimacy, spiritual unity, and financial stewardship.

les1.title: Communication and Conflict Resolution
les1.intro: Healthy communication builds trust. Conflict is inevitable, but resolving it requires listening with humility and love. Couples must speak with truth and grace.
les1.verse1: Everyone should be quick to listen, slow to speak, and slow to anger.
les1.verse2: Do not let any unwholesome talk come out of your mouths, but only what is helpful for building others up.
les1.verse3: A gentle answer turns away wrath.
les1.q1: How can you improve the way you speak and listen to your spouse?
les1.q2: Write a goal to communicate with more grace this week. What will you change in your attitude or words?

les2.title: Forgiveness and Grace
les2.intro: Forgiveness heals the wounds of marriage. Just as Christ forgives us, we must forgive each other to restore unity and peace.
les2.verse1: Forgive as the Lord forgave you.
les2.verse2: Be kind and compassionate to one another, forgiving each other.
les2.verse3: Not seven times, but seventy-seven times.
les2.q1: Is there something you need to forgive or ask forgiveness for today?
les2.q2: Pray together and ask each other for forgiveness if needed. Note how this brought you peace and unity.

les3.title: Roles and Responsibilities in Marriage
les3.intro: Biblical roles reflect the love and service of Christ. The husband leads with love, the wife supports with respect. Both serve each other with humility.
les3.verse1: Husbands, love your wives, just as Christ loved the church.
les3.verse2: Live with your wives in an understanding way.
les3.verse3: I will make a helper suitable for him.
les3.q1: How are you serving one another? Do you honor your roles with love?
les3.q2: Thank God for your spouse and write each other a note of affirmation for their work in the home.

mod2.title: Part 2: Intimacy, Spiritual Unity and Stewardship

les4.title: Sexual Intimacy and Purity
les4.intro: Sexual intimacy is sacred and unites the couple. It must be cultivated with love, respect and purity. It reflects the marriage covenant.
les4.verse1: Marriage should be honored by all, and the marriage bed kept pure.
les4.verse3: Rejoice with the wife of your youth.
les4.q1: How can you strengthen your intimacy and mutual trust?
les4.q2: Have an open and loving conversation about your needs and expectations in intimacy.

les5.title: Unity in Spiritual Growth and Purpose
les5.intro: Spiritual unity strengthens marriage. Couples who pray and serve God together grow in love and purpose.
les5.verse1: A cord of three strands is not quickly broken.
les5.verse2: Seek first his kingdom and his righteousness.
les5.verse3: As for me and my household, we will serve the Lord.
les5.q1: What place does God have in your daily marriage?
les5.q2: Set a time to pray together each day this week and note what God shows you.

les6.title: Stewardship and Finances
les6.intro: Finances often cause tension in marriage. God calls couples to be generous, avoid debt, and manage with wisdom and unity.
les6.verse1: You cannot serve both God and money.
les6.verse2: The plans of the diligent lead to profit.
les6.verse3: The love of money is a root of all kinds of evil.
les6.q1: Are you managing your money with wisdom and purpose?
les6.q2: Review your budget together and make a commitment of faithfulness in your finances before God.

guia_decision_matrimonio.title: Biblical Guide: The Marriage Decision
guia_decision_matrimonio.desc: A 10-lesson study on the most common traps when choosing a spouse. A biblical and practical guide for making wise decisions about marriage.

les1.title: Trap #1: Too Fast!
les1.intro: Marrying quickly out of fear, loneliness, or a desire to be loved is dangerous. Perfect love drives out fear.
les1.verse2: God works in you to will and to act in order to fulfill his good purpose.
les1.q1: What warning signs can I identify from this trap in my own life or relationship?
les1.q2: How can I practice the patience of Psalm 37:7 this week while evaluating my spiritual and emotional maturity?

les2.title: Trap #2: Too Young!
les2.intro: Marrying before developing personal identity and life goals leads to frustration. To love is to obey, not just to feel.
les2.verse1: To love is to obey, not just to feel.
les2.verse2: The testing of your faith produces perseverance.
les2.q1: Do I have clarity about who I am in Christ and what my spiritual, personal and family goals are?
les2.q2: Write down your spiritual and personal goals before making a commitment.

les3.title: Trap #3: Too Eager to Marry
les3.intro: The rush to marry often hides emotional pain or insecurity. Only Jesus can satisfy the soul.
les3.q1: What am I trying to avoid or fix by rushing into marriage?
les3.q2: What concrete steps can I take to heal my inner life before committing my future?

les4.title: Trap #4: To Please Others
les4.intro: Marrying to please others ends in resentment. Each person will give an account to God, not to others.
les4.verse1: Each of us will give an account to God.
les4.verse2: Listen to advice and accept instruction.
les4.q1: Am I making this decision out of obedience to God or pressure from others?
les4.q2: Make a list of 10 things you are looking for in a spouse and 10 things you would not tolerate.

les5.title: Trap #5: Too Little Shared Life Experience
les5.intro: Not sharing diverse experiences prevents deeply knowing the other person. Premature physical intimacy clouds judgment.
les5.verse1: Do not make friends with a hot-tempered person.
les5.q1: Have we resolved conflicts together? Have we gotten to know each other in diverse situations?
les5.q2: How can we delay physical intimacy to truly get to know each other first?

les6.title: Trap #6: Unrealistic Expectations
les6.intro: Expecting your partner to heal your wounds or change after marriage is dangerous. Only God transforms.
les6.verse2: Put childish ways behind you before marriage.
les6.q1: What are my expectations about marriage? Are they realistic or idealized?
les6.q2: How can I let love motivate growth rather than cover unresolved pain?

les7.title: Trap #7: Unresolved Personal Problems
les7.intro: Problems not addressed before marriage will get worse over time. Control, addictions, anger or jealousy do not disappear on their own.
les7.q1: Are there behaviors in me or my partner (control, anger, jealousy, addictions) that need healing before marriage?
les7.q2: Forgiveness is given, but trust is earned with time and fruit. How do I apply this?

les8.title: Trap #8: Minimizing Spiritual Compatibility
les8.intro: Spiritual differences will cause deep conflict. Do not be unequally yoked.
les8.verse1: Do not be unequally yoked with unbelievers.
les8.q1: What is the real spiritual maturity of my partner? Do we share the same values and priorities in Christ?
les8.q2: Am I willing not to settle for less than what God wants to give me?

les9.title: Trap #9: No Communication Rules
les9.intro: Poor communication ruins the relationship before it starts. It is essential to establish a healthy style from the beginning of dating.
les9.verse2: If it is possible, as far as it depends on you, live at peace with everyone.
les9.q1: How do we communicate when there is disagreement? Do we listen without interrupting and speak with respect?
les9.q2: What specific communication rules can we establish from today in our relationship?

les10.title: Final Reflection: Love-Kindness vs. Fear-Kindness
les10.intro: Love-kindness is based on truth, trust and freedom. Fear-kindness is based on fear, control and manipulation. Love drives out fear.
les10.q1: Which of these 9 traps have I seen or experienced personally?
les10.q2: How am I growing in emotional and spiritual maturity?
les10.q3: Am I looking for a spouse to complete me or to complement me?
les10.q4: What specific boundaries do I need to set to protect my future and honor God in my relationships?

manual_estudiante.title: Student Manual: Christian Leadership
manual_estudiante.desc: A 16-week program to develop integral Christian leaders, grounded in the Word, empowered by the Spirit, and committed to the mission of the Kingdom.

les1.title: Foundations of Christian Leadership
les1.verse1: Trust in the Lord with all your heart and lean not on your own understanding; in all your ways submit to him, and he will make your paths straight.
les1.verse2: Whoever wants to become great among you must be your servant... just as the Son of Man did not come to be served, but to serve.
les1.verse3: An overseer must be above reproach, faithful to his wife, temperate, self-controlled, respectable, hospitable, able to teach.
les1.objectives: Objectives: (1) Understand the biblical foundations of Christian leadership. (2) Contrast servant leadership with worldly authority. (3) Evaluate personal character according to biblical qualifications.
les1.q1: What stands out to you about Jesus' definition of greatness in Matthew 20?
les1.q2: Which of the character traits in 1 Timothy 3 do you find most challenging?
les1.q3: How can you begin to practice biblical leadership in your current context?
les1.q4: Application: Identify a discreet way to serve others this week without seeking recognition. Then write in your journal: What arose in your heart while you served? Daily question: What is God teaching me about leading from the heart — and not from the spotlight?

les1.title: The Holy Spirit in Leadership
les1.verse1: You will receive power when the Holy Spirit comes on you; and you will be my witnesses to the ends of the earth.
les1.verse2: The fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, forbearance, kindness, goodness, faithfulness, gentleness, and self-control.
les1.verse3: Those who are led by the Spirit of God are the children of God.
les1.objectives: Objectives: (1) Understand how the Holy Spirit empowers Christian leadership. (2) Identify the difference between spiritual gifts and spiritual fruit. (3) Learn to listen to the Spirit's guidance in daily leadership decisions.
les1.q1: What role does the Holy Spirit play in your daily leadership?
les1.q2: What fruit of the Spirit do you notice God developing in you?
les1.q3: Are there moments where you perceive the Spirit's correction or direction?
les1.q4: Application: Ask the Holy Spirit to guide you in a ministry decision this week. Note what you hear: a passage, a conviction, or an impression. Act on that guidance and reflect with a mentor. Daily question: Where is God inviting me to depend more on His Spirit than on my own strength?

les1.title: The Gospel Defines Leadership
les1.verse1: If I, your Lord and Teacher, have washed your feet, you also should wash one another's feet. I have set you an example.
les1.verse2: Whoever wants to become great among you must be your servant... even the Son of Man did not come to be served, but to serve.
les1.verse3: Have the same mindset as Christ Jesus, who made himself nothing by taking the very nature of a servant.
les1.objectives: Objectives: (1) Discover how the Gospel defines and transforms leadership. (2) Understand humility as the foundation of Christian influence. (3) Identify practical ways to lead through sacrificial service.
les1.q1: How does Jesus challenge your motives for leading?
les1.q2: Where are you resisting the Gospel's call to serve?
les1.q3: What would leading more like Jesus look like this week?
les1.q4: Application: Serve someone without being asked this week. Reflect on how that changed your perspective on leadership. Daily question: What is God revealing about my pride, my motives, and my posture as a leader?

les1.title: The Great Commission Mandate
les1.verse1: Go and make disciples of all nations... teaching them to obey everything I have commanded you. And surely I am with you always, to the very end of the age.
les1.verse2: You will receive power when the Holy Spirit comes on you; and you will be my witnesses to the ends of the earth.
les1.verse3: How beautiful are the feet of those who bring good news!
les1.objectives: Objectives: (1) Understand the Great Commission as a personal and communal calling. (2) Identify the Holy Spirit's role in evangelism. (3) Develop confidence and clarity to share the gospel.
les1.q1: What fears do you have about sharing your faith?
les1.q2: How can the Spirit help you be a bold and humble witness?
les1.q3: Who in your life needs to hear the gospel?
les1.q4: Application: Pray for a specific opportunity to share the gospel this week. Prepare with a story or verse. Be sensitive to the Holy Spirit's timing and respond in obedience. Daily question: Who is God sending me to? What steps of obedience is He calling me to take?

les1.title: Our Identity in Christ
les1.verse1: We are God's handiwork, created in Christ Jesus to do good works, which God prepared in advance for us to do.
les1.verse2: Before I formed you in the womb I knew you, before you were born I set you apart; I appointed you as a prophet to the nations.
les1.verse3: He has saved us and called us to a holy life — not because of anything we have done but because of his own purpose and grace.
les1.objectives: Objectives: (1) Understand that our identity is in Christ, not in performance. (2) Recognize that calling begins with 'being' before 'doing'. (3) Explore gifts, passions, and contexts as signs of purpose.
les1.q1: Where have you been trying to define yourself by your activity rather than your identity in Christ?
les1.q2: What evidence do you see that God has designed you with purpose?
les1.q3: What might be a first step of obedience to your calling?
les1.q4: Application: Write a personal statement of identity in Christ and purpose in the Kingdom. Share with your mentor or small group. Daily question: Am I living from my identity in Christ or trying to earn it by what I do?

les1.title: Spiritual Disciplines and Transformation
les1.verse1: Do not conform to the pattern of this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your mind, so that you can discern what God's will is.
les1.verse2: Train yourself to be godly. For physical training is of some value, but godliness has value for all things.
les1.verse3: Remain in me, as I also remain in you. No branch can bear fruit by itself; it must remain in the vine.
les1.objectives: Objectives: (1) Understand spiritual formation as continuous transformation. (2) Explore spiritual disciplines as tools for communion with God. (3) Evaluate personal areas of spiritual growth.
les1.q1: What habits are shaping you more than the Word of God?
les1.q2: What spiritual disciplines help you most to remain in Christ?
les1.q3: Where are you resisting God's formative process?
les1.q4: Application: Choose a spiritual discipline this week (prayer, fasting, Bible reading, silence, service). Practice it intentionally and reflect: What did you notice about God and yourself? Daily question: What is shaping me more: the world or the Spirit?

les1.title: Leadership in Community
les1.verse1: A new command I give you: Love one another. By this everyone will know that you are my disciples.
les1.verse2: Be completely humble and gentle; be patient, bearing with one another in love... to keep the unity of the Spirit through the bond of peace.
les1.verse3: As iron sharpens iron, so one person sharpens another.
les1.objectives: Objectives: (1) Understand that spiritual leadership is lived in community. (2) Develop skills for healthy relationships in ministry. (3) Learn to give and receive correction in love.
les1.q1: Are you leading from relationships or from functions?
les1.q2: Who do you need to forgive or reach out to this week?
les1.q3: How can you build more authentic ministerial relationships?
les1.q4: Application: Take the initiative to talk with someone you have been avoiding. Practice listening without defending and responding with humility. Write a prayer for unity and healthy relationships in your team. Daily question: Am I leading from love and unity, or from control and isolation?

semana8.title: Week 8: Leadership in the Local Church

les1.title: The Body of Christ in Action
les1.verse1: They devoted themselves to the apostles' teaching and to fellowship, to the breaking of bread and to prayer... And the Lord added to their number daily those who were being saved.
les1.verse2: Christ himself gave the apostles, the prophets, the evangelists, the pastors and teachers, to equip his people for works of service.
les1.verse3: Be shepherds of God's flock that is under your care, watching over them... not lording it over those entrusted to you, but being examples to the flock.
les1.objectives: Objectives: (1) Understand the purpose of the local church as body and community. (2) Identify leadership roles in the church according to the New Testament. (3) Evaluate how to serve and grow within the local church.
les1.q1: How can you honor and support the leadership of your local church?
les1.q2: Where do you see opportunities to serve with your gifts and passion?
les1.q3: What prevents you from committing more deeply to your church?
les1.q4: Application: Attend your local church faithfully this week. Ask a leader how you can help in a practical way. Pray for your pastors and write an encouraging note for one of them. Daily question: Am I viewing the local church as a task or as an essential part of my calling?

les1.title: The Way of the Servant
les1.verse1: Whoever wants to become great among you must be your servant... even the Son of Man did not come to be served, but to serve and to give his life as a ransom.
les1.verse2: If I, your Lord and Teacher, have washed your feet, you also should wash one another's feet. I have set you an example.
les1.verse3: Do nothing out of selfish ambition or vain conceit. Rather, in humility value others above yourselves.
les1.objectives: Objectives: (1) Understand service as the path of biblical leadership. (2) Identify attitudes that reflect a servant heart. (3) Develop practical actions of servant leadership.
les1.q1: Which aspect of Jesus' example of service do you resist most?
les1.q2: Who can you serve this week in a tangible way?
les1.q3: How does your heart change when you serve without recognition?
les1.q4: Application: Choose a service task you would normally avoid. Do it with joy, without telling anyone. Write how that experience impacted your perspective on leadership. Daily question: Am I leading to be seen, or so that others may be blessed?

les1.title: Public Life and Private Life
les1.verse1: The integrity of the upright guides them, but the unfaithful are destroyed by their duplicity.
les1.verse2: I will walk in my house with blameless heart. I will not look with approval on anything that is vile.
les1.verse3: In everything set them an example by doing what is good. Show integrity, seriousness and soundness of speech.
les1.objectives: Objectives: (1) Define integrity biblically and its role in leadership. (2) Identify personal areas that need consistency between private and public life. (3) Establish practices to cultivate a life of integrity.
les1.q1: Where do you need to align your private life with what you teach publicly?
les1.q2: What accountability measures can you take this week?
les1.q3: How does your integrity affect others' trust in you?
les1.q4: Application: Choose an area where you can strengthen your integrity (use of time, media, relationships, money). Ask for prayer from a trusted mentor or friend. Evaluate how small changes can have a big impact in the long run. Daily question: Am I living in a way that honors God even when no one sees me?

les1.title: Confrontation and Reconciliation
les1.verse1: If your brother or sister sins, go and point out their fault, just between the two of you. If they listen to you, you have won them over.
les1.verse2: Do not repay anyone evil for evil. If it is possible, as far as it depends on you, live at peace with everyone.
les1.verse3: In your anger do not sin. Do not let the sun go down while you are still angry, and do not give the devil a foothold.
les1.objectives: Objectives: (1) Understand the biblical model of confrontation and reconciliation. (2) Develop skills for resolving conflicts with grace and truth. (3) Evaluate personal attitudes that fuel conflict.
les1.q1: How do you usually react to conflict? Do you avoid it, confront it, or go silent?
les1.q2: What relationship in your life needs a restorative conversation?
les1.q3: How can you cultivate peace instead of division?
les1.q4: Application: Think of someone you need to reconcile with or forgive. Pray for wisdom and humility to approach them in love. Take the first step toward peace, trusting the Spirit's guidance. Daily question: Am I reflecting Christ's character in the way I handle conflicts?

les1.title: Wisdom from Above
les1.verse1: If any of you lacks wisdom, you should ask God, who gives generously to all without finding fault, and it will be given to you.
les1.verse2: Blessed are those who find wisdom... She is a tree of life to those who take hold of her.
les1.verse3: Give your servant a discerning heart to govern your people and to distinguish between right and wrong.
les1.objectives: Objectives: (1) Understand wisdom as a spiritual gift and daily practice. (2) Distinguish between human wisdom and wisdom from above. (3) Learn to make decisions based on biblical principles.
les1.q1: In what areas do you urgently need God's wisdom?
les1.q2: Do you tend to react according to your emotions or seek spiritual direction?
les1.q3: What wise counsel have you received that you need to apply?
les1.q4: Application: Pray daily this week asking for specific wisdom in your leadership. Read a chapter of Proverbs each day and note a practical principle. Consult a wise leader before making an important decision. Daily question: Am I making decisions with wisdom from above, or just with human logic?

les1.title: Discipleship and Reproduction
les1.verse1: And the things you have heard me say in the presence of many witnesses entrust to reliable people who will also be qualified to teach others.
les1.verse2: Go and make disciples of all nations... teaching them to obey everything I have commanded you.
les1.verse3: Choose seven men from among you who are known to be full of the Spirit and wisdom. We will turn this responsibility over to them.
les1.objectives: Objectives: (1) Understand leadership as a multiplying mission. (2) Identify and disciple others to reproduce faithful leaders. (3) Delegate with wisdom, vision, and trust in the Holy Spirit.
les1.q1: Are you leading in a way that allows others to be trained and sent?
les1.q2: What prevents you from delegating more ministry to others?
les1.q3: Who are you investing in this season?
les1.q4: Application: Make a list of 1–2 people you can begin to train or disciple. Meet with one of them this week and pray for their growth. Ask the Lord for strategies to raise up new leaders. Daily question: Am I leading for the moment or for the movement?

les1.title: Spiritual Resilience
les1.verse1: We are hard pressed on every side, but not crushed; perplexed, but not in despair; persecuted, but not abandoned; struck down, but not destroyed.
les1.verse2: Do not be anxious about anything, but in every situation, by prayer and petition, with thanksgiving... the peace of God will guard your hearts and your minds.
les1.verse3: God is our refuge and strength, an ever-present help in trouble. Therefore we will not fear.
les1.objectives: Objectives: (1) Recognize that pressure is part of the calling to leadership. (2) Learn to respond with faith, prayer, and dependence on the Spirit. (3) Identify practices to cultivate spiritual resilience.
les1.q1: What recent situations have put pressure on your leadership?
les1.q2: How do you usually react: in faith or frustration?
les1.q3: What do you need to change in order to remain firm under pressure?
les1.q4: Application: Bring your burdens to the Lord in prayer this week. Share with a mature mentor what is overwhelming you. Practice rest as an act of faith. Daily question: Am I trying to lead with my own strength or from dependence on God?

les1.title: Stewardship and Generosity
les1.verse1: Whoever can be trusted with very little can also be trusted with much. So if you have not been trustworthy with worldly wealth, who will trust you with true riches?
les1.verse2: Whoever sows generously will also reap generously... God is able to bless you abundantly.
les1.verse3: No one can serve two masters... You cannot serve both God and money.
les1.objectives: Objectives: (1) Understand money management as a spiritual and leadership issue. (2) Identify biblical principles for stewardship and generosity. (3) Practice financial decisions that reflect faith and faithfulness.
les1.q1: What place does money have in your life and leadership?
les1.q2: How can you demonstrate financial faithfulness in this stage of your life?
les1.q3: Where can you grow in generosity?
les1.q4: Application: Track your expenses this week as a spiritual practice. Pray about a specific way to give generously. Consider supporting a ministry or local need as an act of faith. Daily question: Am I using my resources to advance the Kingdom, or just my own comfort?

semana16.title: Week 16: Perseverance and Finishing with Purpose

les1.title: Running with Eyes on the Goal
les1.verse1: Let us throw off everything that hinders... and let us run with perseverance the race marked out for us, fixing our eyes on Jesus, the pioneer and perfecter of faith.
les1.verse2: I have fought the good fight, I have finished the race, I have kept the faith. Now there is in store for me the crown of righteousness.
les1.verse3: Let us not become weary in doing good, for at the proper time we will reap a harvest if we do not give up.
les1.objectives: Objectives: (1) Value perseverance as an essential virtue of Christian leadership. (2) Prepare to faithfully finish the ministerial journey. (3) Renew vision with focus on Christ and eternal reward.
les1.q1: What obstacles threaten to make you give up in your leadership?
les1.q2: How does it encourage you to know that Jesus already ran and overcame for you?
les1.q3: What legacy of faithfulness do you want to leave?
les1.q4: Application: Write a letter to yourself about why you started leading. Read that letter when you face discouragement. Share your letter with a mentor or companion so they can pray with you. Daily question: Am I running with my eyes on the eternal goal or on temporal distractions?
